threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus is the quickest way to get anywhere; locals know the hidden shortcuts through the parks.
Heard a couple arguing on the N87 last night; she threw a half-eaten kebab at him. Classic Brixton Friday vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Hearing a street musician playing a remix of Turkish pop while the smell of döner wafted through the air on Karl-Marx-Straße.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a street vendor selling currywurst with vegan options while a drum circle grooves just a block away. That's Berlin for you.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the smell of churros at 16th and Mission is a warning—locals are fueling up before the chaos of an overstuffed train.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 last week, driver stopped to help a guy untangle his shopping cart from the Muni wires. No rush, just a moment of humanity.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the Gaudí hype; they miss the little plaça where locals gather to chat away the afternoon.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their baffled face when the tram loses power and everyone just shrugs it off like it’s normal.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' when everyone knows it's just the 'G' line here. You'll get used to the lingo, eventually.
Caught a glimpse of two guys in suits deep in a debate about the best kebab joints at 3 AM. Brixton’s late-night life never sleeps.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a suit next to a street artist spraying graffiti, both laughing at the same joke. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a kiosk where you can grab a beer and a döner at 2 AM, all while standing next to a guy playing the accordion.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to carry an extra pair of socks after a night out; those potholes can suddenly turn into puddles when it rains.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 at Civic Center yesterday, and just as we rolled out, a guy in a full clown suit hopped on. No one batted an eye.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the loudest bars are the most fun, but the best moments are often in the quiet plazas where locals share a simple beer and laughter.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still scan menus for English options at local tapas bars, missing the joy of ordering a surprise dish from the waiter.
